texto: The "you only live once" theory: Many people say that they do not believe in reincarnation; they believe when you die, you're dead. What about fish, clams, horses, bears, flies? People that say you only live once are saying some souls get to live just one life as a worm, and that is it for all of eternity.Other people believe you are judged by a god after you die. Is the worm judged?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Michael Smith que provoca sentido da existência e experiência vivida. Ao questionar “The "you only live once" theory: Many people say that they do not believe in reincarnation; they …”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, atheism, fairness, com foco em fairness e god.

texto: You cannot control life, but you can change the way you see life.Animals other than man are more fulfilled, because they have less mind blocking life, but they are stuck with the perspective and perception they are born with. We (human beings) can greatly improve our perception by learning the ultimate truth.All the problems we have stem from people not knowing the truth of life.
explicacao: Citação de Michael Smith sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “You cannot control life, but you can change the way you see life.Animals other than man are more …” articula reflexao, animals, evolution em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como mudanca e evolution.

texto: Everything is balanced. Everything physical (matter/energy) goes back and forth in balanced circles, cycles, or the equivalent. Birth-death, old-young, big-small, strong-weak, start-stop, up-down, rich-poor, beginning-end, fast-slow, hot-cold, pain-pleasure, win-lose, day-night, full-empty, high-low, in-out, success-failure, united-divided, give-receive, creation-destruction, on-off, positive-negative, etc.Positive and negative forces moving in balance are the physical universe.
explicacao: Citação de Michael Smith sobre esforço, persistência e resultados. “Everything is balanced. Everything physical (matter/energy) goes back and forth in balanced circl…” articula sucesso, balance, cycles em registro de sucesso, com relevância para contextos como boa-noite e luto.
